ev.headline: Hyundai Unveils IONIQ V at Beijing Auto Show 2026
ev.summary: Hyundai launched the IONIQ V electric liftback in Beijing with over 600 km CLTC range on an 800V platform, marking the beginning of an aggressive 20-model offensive in China's EV market.

ev.headline: CATL Super Tech Day 2026: Breakthrough Battery Technologies Unveiled
ev.summary: CATL announced next-generation solid-state battery architectures with higher energy density, improved safety, and longer lifespan, enabling faster charging and more affordable EV production.

ev.headline: Rivian Starts R2 Production Ahead of Customer Deliveries
ev.summary: Rivian began producing its new R2 all-electric SUV at its Illinois plant, with the $58,000 performance model featuring 330-mile range and customer deliveries scheduled for later this spring.

ev.headline: Beijing Auto Show 2026: Future of Auto Industry is Electric and Chinese
ev.summary: A glimpse at the Beijing Auto Show reveals over 17 massive halls displaying predominantly electric vehicles, with Chinese automakers dominating while Tesla notably skipped the show for the third consecutive year.

ev.headline: U.S. News Announces 2026 Best Hybrid and Electric Cars Awards
ev.summary: 19 models recognized with 15 newly expanded categories including awards to Hyundai and Kia leading the industry with three awards each, as consumer interest in hybrids increases despite end of EV incentives.

ev.headline: Volvo EX60 Production Underway with Strong European Demand
ev.summary: Volvo began EX60 production at its Swedish plant with customer deliveries set for early summer 2026, as European order volumes exceed internal forecasts.

ev.headline: ABB E-mobility Launches M-Series Modular DC Charging Platform
ev.summary: ABB introduced the M-Series, a modular air-cooled charging platform scaling from 200 kW to 1.2 MW, offering flexible upgrades across public, retail, and fleet charging sites.

ev.headline: Chevrolet Bolt 2027 Model Begins Shipping with Native NACS Port
ev.summary: The 2027 Chevrolet Bolt started shipping from GM's plant with 262-mile EPA range, $28,995 starting price, native NACS charging port, and available Super Cruise feature.

ev.headline: XPeng Announces Romania as Its 61st Global Market
ev.summary: Chinese automaker XPeng expanded internationally with Romania becoming its 61st market worldwide, following recent expansion into Mexico.
ev.source: EV | The latest news on the electric vehicle industry

ev.headline: Rivian Patents Wall-Mounted Electronics Unit for Vehicle-to-Home Power
ev.summary: Rivian filed a patent for a wall-mounted device allowing its EVs to power homes, enabling bidirectional energy flow between vehicles and residential systems.
ev.source: EV | The latest news on the electric vehicle industry

ev.headline: Porsche Cayenne Coupé Electric Launches at Beijing Auto Show 2026
ev.summary: Porsche premiered the Cayenne Coupé Electric with three variants including the 850 kW Turbo model delivering up to 669 km WLTP range.

ev.headline: Renault 5 Turbo 3E Gets In-Wheel Motors from Protean Electric
ev.summary: Renault confirmed Protean Electric as supplier for in-wheel motors on the Renault 5 Turbo 3E, making it the first European passenger car to enter production with integrated wheel motors.

ev.headline: BYD Denza Z Electric Hypercar Headed to Europe This Summer
ev.summary: BYD's Denza Z, launched at Beijing Auto Show as an intelligent electric supercar with 1,000+ hp, is set for European availability this summer to compete with luxury automakers.

ev.headline: CUPRA Raval UK Pricing Confirmed for Summer 2026 Deliveries
ev.summary: CUPRA set UK pricing for the all-electric Raval starting at £23,785 for the Origin trim and rising to £36,310 for the VZ Extreme, with deliveries in summer 2026.

ev.headline: California EV Sales Drop Sharply to 13.7% Market Share
ev.summary: California EV registrations plummeted to 13.7% in Q1 from 21% in 2025, reflecting federal policy shifts eliminating tax credits and fuel economy mandate enforcement.

ev.headline: US Automakers Reset EV Strategy Amid Policy Uncertainty
ev.summary: GM and Ford pulled back EV investments totaling over $20 billion, with GM reporting $1.6 billion impact and Ford expecting $19.5 billion in charges as federal incentives and mandates end.

ev.headline: Trump Administration Issues CAFE Rule Rollback and EV Policy Reversal
ev.summary: The Trump Administration unveiled the 'Freedom Means Affordable Cars' proposal rolling back fuel economy standards, eliminating EV tax credits, and repealing California emission waivers.

ev.headline: Global EV Sales Hit 4 Million in Q1 2026 Amid Regional Disparities
ev.summary: Q1 2026 global EV sales reached 4 million units, down 3% year-over-year, with Europe surging 37% while US and China showed weakness due to policy changes and reduced demand.

ev.headline: US EV Sales Fall 27% Year-Over-Year in Q1 2026
ev.summary: US electric vehicle sales declined 27% year-over-year to 216,399 units in Q1, with market share at 5.8%, as buyers adjusted to the loss of federal tax incentives.

ev.headline: BYD Emerges as Global EV Volume Leader, Outsells Tesla
ev.summary: BYD sold over 2 million EVs in 2025, edging past Tesla in annual global deliveries and solidifying China's position as the dominant EV manufacturing powerhouse.

ev.headline: Europe's EV Market Hits Record March Sales Amid Oil Price Spike
ev.summary: Europe recorded over 500,000 EV sales in March 2026 for the first time, driven by rising gas prices from Middle East tensions and EU subsidy programs.

ev.headline: Polestar Posts Record 2025 Sales with 34% Growth
ev.summary: Polestar achieved 60,000+ vehicle sales in 2025, a 34% increase year-over-year with 50% revenue growth exceeding $3 billion, driven by expanding global production.

ev.headline: EV Range Improvements Continue in 2026 Models
ev.summary: Average EV range climbed to 325 miles in 2026 models from 293 miles in 2025, with charging speeds accelerating and efficiency gains within vehicle classes despite market shifts toward larger SUVs.

ev.headline: Stellantis Announces $13 Billion US Investment for Manufacturing Expansion
ev.summary: Stellantis declared a record $13 billion investment to expand US operations, planning new EV and range-extended vehicle production with over 900 jobs created by 2028.

ev.headline: Federal EV Tax Credits Expire as Policy Shift Accelerates Hybrid Growth
ev.summary: Federal tax credits for EVs expired in September 2025, causing EV market share to plummet from 10.3% to 5.2% by Q4 while hybrid sales surged 30% amid policy uncertainty.

v2x.headline: PG&E and Tesla Turn Cybertruck into a Grid Asset with First AC V2G Program
v2x.summary: PG&E approved Tesla Cybertruck for its V2X program, marking the first AC-based vehicle-to-grid system in California. Customers can power homes during outages and earn money by selling power to the grid during peak demand.

v2x.headline: Volkswagen and Elli Launch V2G Commercial Offer in Germany
v2x.summary: Volkswagen announced a V2G market launch in Q4 2026, with one million vehicles in Europe already technically prepared for bidirectional charging. The integrated offer includes dynamic tariffs, DC chargers, and energy management systems.

v2x.headline: GM Leads Charge in V2H EVs with 246,000 Vehicles Sold
v2x.summary: General Motors sold over 246,000 V2H-capable vehicles as a top-two EV seller in 2025. GM is exploring leasing programs for home energy systems to make bidirectional charging more accessible to homeowners.

v2x.headline: Rivian and Redwood Deploy Largest Second-Life EV Battery Grid Storage Project
v2x.summary: Rivian partnered with Redwood Materials to deploy 12MW/63MWh grid-scale storage using second-life EV batteries in Nevada. The project supports AI infrastructure and marks the largest second-life battery development globally.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging Reaches Mainstream Adoption in 2026
v2x.summary: EV bidirectional charging technology is finally reaching mainstream adoption in 2026, with EVs functioning as both mobile batteries and power stations. Major automakers now offer V2H and V2G capabilities on new platforms.

v2x.headline: UTC V2H Capable Models Rapidly Expanding with GM Silverado and Ford
v2x.summary: Multiple mainstream EV models now support V2H including Ford F-150 Lightning, Kia EV9, GM Ultium trucks and SUVs. Installation costs remain around $4,000-$10,000 but federal tax credits and utility rebates help reduce upfront expenses.

v2x.headline: EV Battery Grid Integration Enables 22 Billion Euros in Annual Savings by 2040
v2x.summary: Studies indicate V2G technology could save up to €22 billion in annual system costs in Europe by 2040. Homeowners could save up to 780 euros yearly on electricity bills with bidirectional charging capabilities.

v2x.headline: BMW iX3 V2G Commercial Offering Launches in Spring 2026
v2x.summary: BMW presented its first commercial V2G offering for Germany with E.ON at IAA 2025. The iX3 will use the BMW Wallbox Professional and jointly developed software with E.ON starting spring 2026.

v2x.headline: Nissan Commits to Affordable V2G Solutions by 2026
v2x.summary: Nissan is a bidirectional charging pioneer with multiple V2X projects globally. The company targets affordable V2G implementation by 2026 and has demonstrated substantial earnings potential through grid services.

v2x.headline: Tesla V2H Capability Expands with Cybertruck, Model 3, and Model Y
v2x.summary: Tesla expanded V2H capabilities to 2024+ Model 3, Model Y, and Cybertruck with 11.5kW output. The 2026 Model Y Performance officially supports V2L, with bidirectional expansion across fleet by late 2025.

v2x.headline: Virtual Power Plants Transform Grid Stability with EV Battery Aggregation
v2x.summary: Major OEMs committed to delivering V2X-compatible cars in the 2020s decade. NREL predicts EV batteries could provide 32-62 TWh capacity by 2050, meeting most short-term storage needs as early as 2030.

v2x.headline: EV Batteries as Home Backup Power Moves from Concept to Reality in 2026
v2x.summary: Using an EV as home battery backup is now a mainstream option in 2026. Ford F-150 Lightning, Kia EV9, and GM Ultium trucks can power homes for days during outages with proper bidirectional systems installed.

v2x.headline: Nissan Leaf G99 Grid Certification Advances UK V2G Deployment
v2x.summary: Nissan secured the UK's first G99 grid certification for AC-based V2G with the Nissan Leaf. The company plans affordable V2G solutions first in the UK, then expanded across Europe.

v2x.headline: Utrecht Creates World's First Bidirectional EV Region with 500+ Vehicles
v2x.summary: Hyundai and "We Drive Solar" are building Europe's largest factory for bidirectional charging stations in Utrecht. The project includes over 100 Renault 5s with 500 vehicles planned in this innovative shared mobility service.

v2x.headline: Enphase and Emporia Release Bidirectional EV Chargers in 2026
v2x.summary: Enphase announced plans to release bidirectional EV chargers in 2026 with V2H and V2G capabilities for compatible EV models. Multiple manufacturers now offer bidirectional charging products at various price points.

v2x.headline: Global V2X Automotive Market Projected to Reach $29.52 Billion by 2035
v2x.summary: The V2X automotive market is valued at $4.266 billion in 2026 and expected to grow at 24.0% CAGR to reach $29.52 billion by 2035. Over 75 million connected vehicles globally enable V2X functionality.

v2x.headline: Second-Life EV Batteries Power Data Centers and Grid Infrastructure
v2x.summary: Used EV batteries are being repurposed for grid-scale storage in data centers and manufacturing facilities. Redwood Materials created a division focused on second-life EV batteries with signed MOUs with major automakers.

v2x.headline: Smart Home Integration with V2H Systems Optimizes Peak Demand Response
v2x.summary: EV batteries can provide 70-130 kWh capacity for home backup, supporting entire households during outages with proper integration. Smart home panels coordinate between EVs, solar panels, and grid electricity automatically.

v2x.headline: Kia EV9 Enables V2L, V2H, and V2G with Smart Charge App Integration
v2x.summary: Kia expanded bidirectional capabilities across its EV lineup with V2H capability by 2026. The Kia Smart Charge app enables users to charge during lower-cost periods and participate in grid services programs.

v2x.headline: Hyundai Ioniq 5 and V2H Technology Keeps Homes Running During Outages
v2x.summary: Hyundai Ioniq 5 owners used V2H to maintain power during winter storms. Real-world tests show a fully charged 60 kWh battery powers average homes for up to two days during extended outages.

v2x.headline: Mercedes-Benz EV Bidirectional Charging Launches in 2026 with MB.CHARGE Home
v2x.summary: Mercedes-Benz announced bidirectional charging for the new electric GLC in 2026 through MB.CHARGE Home setup with The Mobility House. The automaker is integrating V2X across premium electric vehicle lineup.

v2x.headline: EV Grid Integration Supports 2030 Renewable Energy Goals Across Europe
v2x.summary: Germany curtailed 9,500 GWh of renewable energy recently, equivalent to powering 3 million EVs for a year. V2G technology enables efficient utilization of surplus renewable energy through battery storage in vehicles.
